summary: Russian forces intensified aerial attacks, launching guided bombs over Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk and striking Bila Tserkva with an Oreshnik missile. Ukraine’s air defenses reported downing 246 of 262 Shahed and other UAVs, while Russian artillery bombarded Kramatorsk with FAB‑250s. Frontline skirmishes continue across the east, north and south, with modest territorial gains reported by Russian units.
key_points: Russia launched an Oreshnik missile strike on Bila Tserkva in Kyiv region overnight (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30396).
Ukrainian air defenses shot down 246 of 262 attacking Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and imitation drones across the country (Source: @kpszsu/62715).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aviation bombs on targets in Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk regions (Source: @kpszsu/62724).
Five FAB‑250 guided bombs hit Kramatorsk, causing at least two injuries and damage to 14 multi‑storey buildings and infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/81444).
Ukrainian forces reported advancing up to 1.1 km on several sectors of the Kharkiv front and 900 m on the Sumy front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
Multiple UAV groups were observed moving toward Kyiv, Poltava, Chernihiv and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts, including a high‑altitude UAV over Kyiv reservoir at 9 km (Source: @eRadarrua/93593).
Russian intelligence warned of a possible ballistic weapon deployment from the north‑east (Source: @kpszsu/62677).
Belarusian border forces reported no Russian troop movements near the frontier, though Russian pressure on Belarus was noted (Source: @UkraineNow/81435).

sections.executive_summary: Russia escalated its aerial campaign, striking Bila Tserkva with an Oreshnik missile and deploying guided bombs over Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk. Ukraine’s air defense claimed 246 UAV kills out of 262 attacks, blunting the Shahed swarm. Frontline reports show limited Russian advances in the east and north, while Kramatorsk suffered a FAB‑250 bombardment.

points: Ukrainian air defense units engaged a massive Shahed‑type UAV wave, confirming 246 aircraft destroyed across northern, southern and eastern sectors, limiting damage to nine locations (Source: @kpszsu/62715).
The Armed Forces reported incremental gains on the Kharkiv front, advancing up to 1,100 m on nine sectors and securing villages such as Granov and Karaichnoe (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
Ukrainian special forces eliminated a Russian reconnaissance operative codenamed “Shaman” in Liman, preventing enemy fire correction on frontline positions (Source: @UkraineNow/81445).
Ukrainian ground units repelled a Russian UAV‑driven attack on the Dnipro logistics corridor, reporting no significant infrastructure loss (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
Ukrainian intelligence noted increased UAV activity over Kyiv, including a high‑altitude reconnaissance drone at 9 km over the Kyiv reservoir (Source: @eRadarrua/93593).
Russian tactical aviation released guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk oblasts, targeting infrastructure and troop concentrations (Source: @kpszsu/62724).
An Oreshnik cruise missile struck Bila Tserkva, Kyiv region, causing unspecified damage and civilian impact (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30396).
Five FAB‑250 guided bombs were dropped on Kramatorsk, injuring at least two civilians and damaging 14 multi‑storey structures and a post office (Source: @UkraineNow/81444).
Russian forces warned of potential ballistic missile deployment from the north‑east, indicating a strategic escalation (Source: @kpszsu/62677).
Russian assault units of the “North” Army Group advanced up to 900 m on twenty‑one sectors of the Sumy front, engaging Ukrainian defenders (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
Ukrainian drones were observed heading toward Vasylivka and Pavlohrad in Dnipropetrovsk oblast, suggesting reconnaissance over key logistics nodes (Source: @kpszsu/62722, @kpszsu/62723).
Russian units reported progress northwest of Alexandrovgrad, engaging in forested combat near Lesnoye along the Volchya River (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
Ukrainian forces claimed breakthroughs near Vozdvyzhevka and Komsomolskoe on the East‑Zaporozhye front, supported by aviation and artillery (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
Ukrainian UAV attacks damaged energy infrastructure in nine Kherson districts, leaving the region without power (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
Russian artillery shelled the town of Kramatorsk with FAB‑250s, causing civilian casualties and structural damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81444).
Multiple Shahed UAV groups were detected approaching Kyiv from directions including Orël, Kursk and Bryansk, prompting intensive air‑defence activity (Source: @kpszsu/62715).
Ukrainian reports noted UAVs flying past Horodnia toward Chernihiv and along the Vysokyi corridor, indicating pressure on northern supply routes (Source: @kpszsu/62680).
Russian forces advanced on the Sumy front, gaining up to 900 m on twenty‑one sectors, with skirmishes in Zapselye, Ivolzhansk and Kondratovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
A high‑altitude reconnaissance UAV was sighted over the Kyiv water reservoir at 9 km altitude, suggesting intelligence‑gathering over the capital (Source: @eRadarrua/93593).
Ukrainian air‑defence units intercepted 10 strike UAVs across nine locations in the north, with debris falling in seven areas (Source: @kpszsu/62715).
Russian guided bomb strikes hit Donetsk oblast, with additional UAV activity reported near the front line (Source: @kpszsu/62719).
Ukrainian forces reported continued skirmishes in the southern Kherson region, with UAV attacks disabling power to nine districts (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
Russian artillery activity increased around the city of Kramatorsk, employing FAB‑250 munitions (Source: @UkraineNow/81444).
Ukrainian defensive operations in the Dnipro corridor prevented significant Russian UAV penetration, maintaining supply line integrity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
Ukrainian intelligence noted no major Russian troop movements near the Belarusian border, though pressure on Belarus was observed (Source: @UkraineNow/81435).
Ukraine’s air‑defence reported shooting down 246 of 262 attacking Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and imitation drones, with 10 strike UAVs confirmed hit across nine sites (Source: @kpszsu/62715).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk, marking a shift to precision strike capability (Source: @kpszsu/62724).
Five FAB‑250 guided bombs were dropped on Kramatorsk, demonstrating Russian use of heavier precision munitions against urban targets (Source: @UkraineNow/81444).
Ukrainian forces intercepted multiple UAV groups heading toward Kyiv from the north‑east, including high‑altitude reconnaissance platforms (Source: @eRadarrua/93593).
Russian air‑defence repelled a massive UAV attack near Yaroslavl, indicating simultaneous pressure on Russian airspace (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).

key_developments: Russia launched an Oreshnik cruise missile strike on Bila Tserkva, Kyiv region, marking a high‑precision offensive against a strategic city (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30396).
Ukrainian air‑defence forces downed 246 of 262 attacking Shahed‑type UAVs, limiting Russian aerial pressure across multiple fronts (Source: @kpszsu/62715).
Russian tactical aviation employed guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk oblasts, targeting infrastructure and troop concentrations (Source: @kpszsu/62724).
Five FAB‑250 guided bombs struck Kramatorsk, causing civilian injuries and extensive damage to residential and administrative buildings (Source: @UkraineNow/81444).
Ukrainian units reported advances of up to 1.1 km on the Kharkiv front and 900 m on the Sumy front, indicating limited but notable territorial gains (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
Multiple UAV groups were observed moving toward Kyiv from the north‑east, including a high‑altitude reconnaissance drone over the Kyiv reservoir (Source: @eRadarrua/93593).
Russian intelligence warned of a possible ballistic missile deployment from the north‑east, suggesting an escalation in long‑range strike capability (Source: @kpszsu/62677).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are intensifying precision strike operations, employing cruise missiles, guided bombs and heavier FAB‑250 munitions against urban and logistical targets, while also threatening ballistic missile use from the north‑east. Ukrainian air defenses have proven effective against large UAV swarms, yet the continued influx of Shahed‑type drones and high‑altitude reconnaissance platforms maintains pressure on critical infrastructure. Frontline dynamics show modest Russian advances in the east and north, but Ukrainian counter‑offensives are achieving limited territorial gains, indicating a contested but fluid battlefield.
sections.regional_response: Western allies are vocalizing diplomatic pressure, with EU officials condemning Russian terror tactics and discussing increased sanctions, while Finland offers to mediate peace talks contingent on a Russian ceasefire. Belarusian border authorities report no direct Russian troop movements, yet acknowledge heightened Russian influence aimed at broader regional destabilization. Ukrainian civil society continues fundraising for defense initiatives, reflecting sustained domestic resilience.
sections.intelligence_notes: UAV traffic patterns reveal a coordinated Russian effort to probe Ukrainian airspace from multiple vectors, especially the north‑east corridor toward Kyiv. The deployment of guided aviation bombs suggests a shift toward precision targeting of command‑and‑control nodes in Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk. The Oreshnik missile use indicates Russia's willingness to employ high‑value strike assets against strategic Ukrainian cities, raising the risk of escalation. Monitoring of ballistic missile threat warnings is essential to anticipate potential long‑range attacks.
